message:
feat(tracker): enhance conflict detection with severity levels and filtering

Improve file conflict detection to provide severity classification,
better filtering options, and integration with robot mode status output.

Conflict Detection (internal/tracker/conflicts.go):

Severity Heuristics:
- "critical" severity when >=3 distinct agents modify same file
- "critical" when first/last change within 10 minutes (rapid edits)
- "warning" severity for standard multi-agent file conflicts
- conflictSeverity() encapsulates the classification logic

Improved DetectConflicts():
- Track LastAt timestamp across all changes to a path
- Return changes array with full RecordedFileChange data
- Better agent deduplication and counting

Refactored ConflictsSince():
- Filter changes first, then run DetectConflicts()
- Eliminates duplicate logic with DetectConflictsRecent()
- Consistent session filtering behavior

CLI Enhancements (internal/cli/changes.go):

New Flags:
- --since flag to specify lookback window (e.g., 6h, 30m)
- --limit flag to cap number of conflicts displayed
- Default remains 24h lookback

Display Improvements:
- Show severity badge [WARNING] or [CRITICAL] per conflict
- Color-code severity (error color for critical, warning for others)
- Wider agent column (24 chars) for readability
- Sort conflicts by LastAt descending (most recent first)

Send Command (internal/cli/send.go):
- Display severity markers [warn] or [CRIT] in conflict warnings
- Consistent with ntm conflicts output styling

Robot Mode (internal/robot/robot.go):
- Add Conflicts []tracker.Conflict field to StatusOutput struct
- appendConflicts() populates conflicts from last 30 minutes
- Limited to 20 conflicts max for bounded output
- conflictLimit constant for easy tuning

Test Coverage:
- TestDetectConflicts verifying multi-agent conflict detection
- TestNoConflictSameAgent ensuring single-agent changes aren't flagged
- TestSnapshotDirectory for file tracking snapshots
- TestDetectFileChanges for change detection logic

Example Usage:
  ntm conflicts                    # Show conflicts in last 24h
  ntm conflicts myproject          # Filter to session
  ntm conflicts --since 6h         # Last 6 hours only
  ntm conflicts --since 30m --limit 10  # Recent, limited
  ntm --robot-status | jq '.conflicts'  # Machine-readable

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
